title: Cross-Browser Activity Correlation
description: Evidence spans Chrome and Firefox profiles on the same machine. Build a unified timeline to correlate visits, searches, and tab activity across browsers.

events.explanation: Chrome activity began with a Jira ticket related to a production deployment, anchoring the investigation timeline.

relationships.label: The Jira ticket often motivates related research or preparation in another browser.
Production release review can precede downloading deployment artefacts.

hints: Think about which event usually anchors a deployment-related investigation.
A Jira ticket for a production release may drive related research activity elsewhere.
The Chrome Jira ticket visit should be placed first, before any Firefox research activity begins.

events.description: [Firefox] Google search: "kubernetes rolling update zero downtime"
events.explanation: Firefox shows a technical search consistent with preparing for the deployment referenced in Chrome.

relationships.label: A technical search commonly leads to the referenced documentation page.

hints: Think about what happens when someone wants to learn a new concept for a ticket.
A technical search often precedes opening the supporting documentation.
The Firefox Kubernetes search should be placed right after the Jira ticket visit and before the docs page is opened.

events.explanation: The Firefox search led to official Kubernetes documentation on rolling updates.

relationships.label: Documentation research often continues in parallel with deployment authentication.
Research activity in Firefox can align with later wiki authentication events.

hints: Think about which page usually appears immediately after a documentation search.
Technical documentation pages often follow focused searches.
The Kubernetes docs page should be placed right after the Firefox search and before the Chrome Jira session cookie is set.

events.description: [Chrome] Cookie set: JSESSIONID on jira.techflow.io (expires session)
events.explanation: A fresh Jira session cookie in Chrome indicates re-authentication or session renewal during the deployment review.

relationships.label: Session cookies usually appear after accessing the target application.
An authenticated session often precedes downloading protected artefacts.

hints: Think about which cookie activity reflects returning to a protected application.
Session cookies often indicate an authenticated browser session during deployment review.
The Chrome JSESSIONID cookie should be placed right after the Jira ticket visit and before the deploy config download.

events.explanation: A production deployment configuration was downloaded via Chrome, a critical artefact for the release timeline.

relationships.label: Downloading protected artefacts often follows an authenticated session.
Downloading a deployment configuration often precedes further deployment checklist research.

hints: Think about which download typically follows after authentication on Jira.
Downloading production configuration is a strong indicator of deployment preparation.
The Chrome deploy config download should be placed right after the JSESSIONID cookie and before the wiki checklist search.

events.description: [Chrome] Google search: "techflow internal wiki deployment checklist"
events.explanation: Chrome was used to locate the internal deployment checklist on the company wiki.

relationships.label: A deployment checklist search typically leads to the checklist page.

hints: Think about what a user searches for next when preparing for a deployment.
A deployment checklist search often precedes opening the internal wiki page.
The Chrome checklist search should be placed right after the deploy config download and right before the wiki checklist page is opened.

events.explanation: The internal wiki checklist page was accessed immediately after the Chrome search.

relationships.label: Checklist access in Chrome can correlate with wiki authentication in Firefox.

hints: Think about which page comes right after a deployment checklist search.
A checklist page visit often represents a direct follow-up to a checklist search.
The Chrome wiki checklist visit should be placed right after the search and before the Firefox wiki session cookie is created.

events.description: [Firefox] Cookie set: wiki_session on wiki.techflow.io (HttpOnly, expires 2024-09-21)
events.explanation: Firefox independently authenticated to the wiki, showing parallel cross-browser access to internal resources.

relationships.label: Wiki authentication in Firefox can align with Chrome wiki access.
Wiki session cookies often precede further navigation to protected resources.

hints: Think about which cookie activity reflects an authenticated browser session on the wiki.
Wiki session cookies often follow authentication or page access activity.
The Firefox wiki_session cookie should be placed right after the Chrome wiki visit and before the Firefox Jenkins job page.

events.explanation: Firefox navigated to the Jenkins CI job page, indicating the deployment pipeline was monitored from a second browser.

relationships.label: CI monitoring often follows authentication to supporting resources.
CI job monitoring often precedes saving or downloading related build artefacts.

hints: Think about which page a developer opens to monitor an in-progress deployment.
Monitoring a CI job often reflects continued deployment investigation.
The Firefox Jenkins job page should be placed right after the wiki_session cookie and right before the console log download.

events.explanation: The Jenkins console log was saved in Firefox, completing the cross-browser evidence chain for the deployment event.

relationships.label: Saving a console log often follows monitoring the related CI job page.

hints: Think about which artefact a developer might save after watching a CI job run.
Saving a CI console log often follows monitoring the related job page.
The Firefox Jenkins console log download should be placed last, immediately after the CI job page visit.
